pi_items.source: Web search and Jackson GitHub release notes. StreamWriteConstraints was introduced in Jackson 2.16 (released Nov 15, 2023) as part of issue #1048. The code uses StreamWriteConstraints which is not available in jackson-core 2.15.2. Jackson 2.15 only has StreamReadConstraints. References: https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson/wiki/Jackson-Release-2.16 and https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/pull/1055

Web search confirms StringUtils.truncate was introduced in Apache Commons Lang 3.5. The code uses StringUtils.truncate() which is not available in 3.1. Sources: Apache Commons Lang 3.5 Javadocs and official documentation.

setup_task.sh lines 36-42 explicitly pins jackson-core to 2.9.0 as a dependency that overrides the transitive 2.15.2 from jackson-databind, creating a conflict that prevents compilation of code using StreamWriteConstraints.

setup_task.sh lines 44-48 explicitly specifies commons-lang3 version 3.1, which does not have the StringUtils.truncate method that was added in 3.5. The code comment states 'Version 3.1 does not have StringUtils.truncate (added in 3.5)'

privileged_info_summary: The fix_dependency_conflicts task creates a Maven project with intentional dependency conflicts. The project requires Jackson 2.16 or later (not 2.15 as claimed) because the code uses StreamWriteConstraints, which was first introduced in Jackson 2.16. The current POM pins jackson-core to 2.9.0 (which causes StreamWriteConstraints compilation errors) and commons-lang3 to 3.1 (which lacks the StringUtils.truncate method added in 3.5). The task is synthetic, with source files and POM generated by setup_task.sh.
